input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK (if not already installed)

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# If missing, install via winget:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# See available workloads and their versions dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows, iOS stubs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify it installed
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK components

The MAUI workload brings its own Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths.

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed by the workload
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check the dotnet packs directory
    $androidSdk = (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\dotnet\packs\Microsoft.Android.Sdk*" |
        Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName
}
Write-Host "Android SDK location: $androidSdk"

# Accept all Android SDK licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# List installed Android SDK components
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Discover which API levels are available and install one if needed
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific platform (pick the latest stable, e.g. android-35)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35"
```

## 4. Set up the Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
# Install emulator + system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_7"

# Launch the emulator (later, when ready to test)
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK target (for WinUI 3)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading UWP/WinUI apps)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1

# The Windows App SDK comes with the MAUI workload, but verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"
```

## 6. Create and build a test project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (net9.0-windows TFM)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -p:AdbTarget=-e
```

## 7. Discover the right TFMs dynamically

If you're unsure which target framework monikers your SDK supports:

```powershell
# List all installed Android/Windows packs and their versions
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list

# Inspect what TFMs the template generates (they match your SDK)
(Get-Content HelloMaui/HelloMaui.csproj) | Select-String "TargetFrameworks"
```

## 8. (Optional) Install Visual Studio with MAUI

If you want the full IDE experience:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then modify the installation to add the MAUI workload:
& "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
    modify --installPath "${env:ProgramFiles}\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --quiet
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you install .NET 8 instead.
- The Windows TFM platform version (`10.0.19041.0`) comes from the project template — match what's in your `.csproj`.
- Hyper-V or WHPX should be enabled in Windows Features for fast Android emulation.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
